seo.title: Authentication & Authorization tools directory
seo.description: A developer-first directory of Authentication & Authorization tools and references.

content.intro: A curated directory of authentication providers, libraries, and security tools designed for modern full-stack development, multi-tenant SaaS architecture, and secure session management.

entries.name: Clerk
entries.description: Managed authentication and user management service with pre-built React/Next.js components and hooks.
pros: Provides ready-to-use UI components for login/profile
Deep integration with Next.js middleware and SSR
Built-in support for multi-session handling
cons: High cost per monthly active user at scale
Limited customization of core UI components beyond CSS

entries.name: Auth.js
entries.description: Flexible open-source authentication library for JavaScript frameworks, formerly known as NextAuth.js.
pros: Supports 50+ OAuth providers out of the box
Database agnostic with multiple official adapters
Free to use without vendor lock-in
cons: Complex configuration for non-standard session patterns
Documentation can be fragmented across versions

entries.name: Lucia
entries.description: A lean, database-centric authentication library that focuses on session management rather than pre-built UI.
pros: Gives developers full control over the database schema
No 'magic' abstractions; follows standard web patterns
Extremely lightweight compared to Auth.js
cons: Requires manual implementation of login forms and UI
Smaller ecosystem of community adapters

entries.name: WorkOS
entries.description: Enterprise-grade identity platform specializing in SAML SSO, SCIM, and B2B SaaS user management.
pros: Simplifies complex SAML integrations with enterprise IT
Automated user provisioning via SCIM
Admin Portal for customer self-service SSO setup
cons: Pricing is geared toward high-revenue B2B contracts
Overkill for simple B2C applications

entries.name: Supabase Auth
entries.description: Authentication service built on GoTrue, integrated directly with Supabase's Postgres Row Level Security (RLS).
pros: Direct integration with Postgres RLS for data isolation
Includes email, phone, and social login providers
Can be self-hosted as part of the Supabase stack
cons: Difficult to use as a standalone service without Postgres
JWT-based session management can be rigid

entries.name: Ory Kratos
entries.description: Headless identity server implementing modern security protocols like OIDC, OAuth2, and WebAuthn.
pros: Cloud-native architecture designed for Kubernetes
Supports hardware security keys and biometrics
Clean separation between identity logic and UI
cons: Steep learning curve for initial configuration
Infrastructure management overhead for self-hosting

entries.name: Zitadel
entries.description: Identity management platform built specifically for multi-tenancy and complex organization structures.
pros: Native support for B2B multi-tenant isolation
Detailed audit trails and security logging
API-first design for custom management consoles
cons: Dashboard UI can be overwhelming for simple use cases
Documentation for complex RBAC can be dense

entries.name: Keycloak
entries.description: Open-source identity and access management solution providing SSO, social login, and user federation.
pros: Extensive feature set including user federation (LDAP/AD)
Highly customizable through Java themes and extensions
Industry standard for self-hosted enterprise IAM
cons: High resource consumption (Java/Wildfly base)
Complex to upgrade and maintain in production

entries.name: JWT.io
entries.description: Developer utility for decoding, verifying, and generating JSON Web Tokens for API testing.
pros: Instant visual debugging of token payloads
Supports multiple signature algorithms (HS256, RS256)
Includes a library list for JWT implementation in 20+ languages
cons: Security risk if production secrets are pasted into the browser
No support for encrypted JWTs (JWE)

entries.name: Stytch
entries.description: Developer-first API for passwordless authentication, including magic links, OTPs, and biometrics.
pros: Optimized for conversion with frictionless login flows
Robust fraud prevention and device fingerprinting
Strong B2B organization management features
cons: SDK can be large for simple web implementations
Higher cost for SMS/OTP based authentication
